$comment: Detailed per-phase buy lists derived from docs/build-guide.md. source: guide = stated verbatim in the docs; estimate = derived, sanity-check before buying; measure = site measurement needed first. Edit freely — this file is yours; assumptions lists every derivation judgment call. Rebuild after editing. See CONTENT.md.

items.spec: 2" × 10' stick, ABS DWV (cellular-core fine for drain)
items.note: Covers the under-paver run + riser stub capped 6–8" proud (Step 1). ABS chosen per guide: 'San Diego is traditionally ABS territory' — match it (or transition cement) at tie-in day.

items.spec: 2" DWV long-sweep quarter bend (long-turn 90)
items.note: Turns the run up into the sink bay. Guide: long-sweep 90 OR two 45s — NEVER a tight vent elbow. Buy the sweep; substitute two 2" 45s only if the sweep won't fit the seam geometry. The sweep makes the riser rodding access for the whole run.

items.spec: 2" DWV cap, ABS
items.note: One on the riser (cut to height at Phase 8 trim-out — tall stubs survive construction), one on the yard end at/below grade. Photograph + measure the yard cap to the fence post.

items.spec: 8 oz can, black ABS cement (no primer needed for ABS)
items.note: For all drain joints. If you ever mix ABS/PVC, guide says use transition cement.

items.spec: 2" × 10' stick, Schedule 40 PVC
items.note: One stick yields BOTH 18–24" straight sleeves: gas sleeve (Step 2) + spare chase (Step 3). Guide allows 3" for 'luxurious clearance' on the gas sleeve; 2" ID (~2.07") passes ¾–1" CSST, fittings included. Do NOT lay actual gas pipe.

items.spec: 2" Sch 40 PVC cap
items.note: Both ends of gas sleeve + both ends of spare chase. Leave dry-fit/taped (they come off at pull-through); Sharpie ends 'GAS SLEEVE' and 'SPARE', photograph both runs.

items.spec: Mason line / poly pull twine, 250' spool
items.note: Guide requires a pull string left inside the gas sleeve AND the spare chase; spool spec is mine. Leftover reused for the Phase 1 corner gas chase.

items.spec: All-purpose/paver sand, 50 lb bag
items.note: 'A few inches of sand between each' pipe and to re-bed pulled pavers. Trench order across the seam is law: drain, spare, gas — gas OUTERMOST, nothing touching.

items.spec: ½" PEX-A or PEX-B, red or white coil
items.note: Measure from the existing supply riser, routed LOW and AROUND the fridge bay (never behind the fridge — compressor/exhaust path), to the sink bay; add slack for the low route.

items.spec: Adapter to existing supply pipe type (verify), 2× ½" elbows or bend supports, ½" stub-out/ball valve, crimp or expansion rings to match your tool
items.note: Guide says only 'PEX + fittings'. Verify what the existing water stub is (copper/CPVC/PEX) before picking the adapter.

items.spec: 1½" tubular P-trap (ABS/poly, not chrome brass outdoors) + matching 1½" tailpiece
items.note: Step 6: hard-pipe tailpiece + trap NOW so above-floor plumbing is done once; interim discharge to the jug. Run the trap arm as HIGH as the sink allows (18–20" above pavers) — banked fall budget.

items.spec: 2" DWV × 1½" slip-joint trap adapter, ABS
items.note: Joins the 1½" trap arm to the 2" riser on tie-in day; riser doubles as rodding access once the trap is pulled. Cheap to buy now with the ABS order.

items.spec: 5–7 gal carboy or jerry can
items.note: The interim drain — 'much nicer than an open bucket: no splash, no smell, no mosquitoes.' Empty to a proper drain, not the landscape.

items.spec: Bulkhead tank fitting sized to the trap discharge hose/pipe (typ. 1"–1½")
items.note: Guide specifies a bulkhead fitting in the jug lid/wall; size is my call — match whatever connects the trap outlet to the jug.

items.spec: Sch 40 PVC 'meter offset' fittings, trade size matched to existing stubs (likely ¾" — verify)
items.note: ~$2 each per guide (or heat-gun-bend your own). Count = number of pipes in the stubs — confirm what the second pipe in the double stub is BEFORE demo; it changes the plan if it's not a spare.

items.spec: Sch 40 PVC coupling, same trade size as stubs
items.note: Glue onto each cut-down stub (leave a coupling's worth above the pavers) before the offset goes on. Step 0.2.4.

items.spec: Push-on caps or tape plugs, same trade size
items.note: Cap the open conduit ends until Phase 2.

items.spec: Small can each, rated for electrical PVC
items.note: Serves Phase 0 offsets and the Phase 2 conduit runs.

items.spec: Pressure-treated 2×4, above-ground UC3A tag, 8' (length assumed — take a cut list from the footprint)
items.note: Guide count is 8–10. Every wall line incl. backsplash wall and kamado cradle; short chorded segments (or band-sawn curve) under the arc. Drill ~¼"-oversize holes mid-board where conduit rises — stronger than a notch.

items.spec: Cedar shim contractor pack (composite shims = the rot-proof upgrade the guide names)
items.note: Shim to the laser at every bearing point, packing UP from the pavers' high point — never grind down.

items.spec: Loctite PL Premium, 10 oz cartridge
items.note: Guide: 'PL Premium ×2'. Bed every sill run before anchoring.

items.spec: ¼" × 3¼" hex-head Tapcon, 25-count box w/ matched masonry bit (length derived: 1½" PT + ≥1" paver embedment)
items.note: Guide says 'Tapcons/paver anchors every few feet' — ~25 covers ~60 LF of sill at ~3' spacing. NO anchor ever lands on the marked drain or gas-sleeve lines — they sit at exactly bedding depth (Phase −1 anchor-conflict warning).

items.spec: Closed-cell polyethylene sill seal, 3½" × 50' roll
items.note: Non-negotiable isolation over the TOP of every PT run before any steel touches it — ACQ/copper-azole PT corrodes galvanized steel. Buy 2 rolls to cover all runs; return one if the LF comes in under 50'.

items.spec: 3-5/8" × 10' 20-gauge EQ galvanized studs — label reads -18/-19/-20/-23 mil; AVOID -14/-16
items.note: 10-footers yield three ~33" studs per stick (locked cut length 32-13/16"). Buy from J&B (El Cajon) or Westside (Sorrento Valley) supply yards, not HD. Dry-stack one test sandwich to 34-13/16" before gang-cutting fifty.

items.spec: 3-5/8" × 10' 20-gauge EQ track (U-channel, no return lips)
items.note: Don't mix up with studs. Curved corner track: snip both flanges every 1–2" and bend — comes out of this count. Blocking/ledgers/boxed members also cut from the stud+track sticks — no separate purchase.

items.spec: 500-count box
items.note: Stud↔track, ~4 per stud. Buy online/Fastenal, not HD. Heads-up (my math, flagged): 150 cut studs × 4 = 600 — grab a second box if you cut all 50 sticks.

items.spec: 100-count box; G185 hot-dip or stainless coating — plain zinc never touches PT
items.note: Track→PT sill; they bite the wood better. The G185/stainless rule at PT is law.

items.spec: Cold-galv zinc spray, standard rattle can
items.note: EVERY cut edge gets sprayed — non-negotiable outdoors. Also every deburred track weep hole (Step 1.3).

items.item: PVC pipe for corner gas chase
items.spec: 1¼"–1½" Sch 40 PVC × 10' (guide's stated size range)
items.note: Step 1.4b: route through the kamado corner's stud knockouts while framing is bare, both ends emerging in flanking bays. ~$8 of pipe; once the Big Joe loads the cradle this corner is blind forever.

items.spec: Cap to match chase pipe size
items.note: Cap both chase ends, insert pull string (Phase −1 spool), label 'GAS CHASE'.

items.spec: 1"×1" × 1/8" aluminum angle, 8' lengths
items.note: Step 1.6: bolt across the hinge-side stud at EVERY door opening NOW — a thin stud web won't hold a hinge for 20 years, miserable retrofit. Count door openings for LF. Guide's alternative: boxed heavier-gauge stud from track offcuts.

items.spec: ¾" Sch 40 gray electrical PVC × 10' (size assumed — must match the existing stubs; verify before buying)
items.note: Conduit runs the WHOLE way — island interior is a damp/wet location: THWN-2 in conduit, no Romex, no bare UF. Measure both circuit paths through stud bays and knockouts.

items.spec: ¾": 4–6 sweep 90s, ~10 couplings, 8–10 male terminal adapters (2 per box entry), LB only if a run needs it
items.note: Guide names offsets (bought Phase 0) and generic 'fittings'; this assortment is my derivation for two circuits and five boxes.

items.spec: Plastic snap bushings sized to the 1½" stud knockouts + conduit KOs, bag of ~20
items.note: Guide: 'bushings/grommets on any cut steel edge.' Grommets at every knockout regardless — abrasion protection is a separate rule from strike protection (D19).

items.spec: Single-gang PVC weatherproof ('Bell') boxes with ¾" hubs
items.note: Grill: low 8–12" in adjacent cavity; fridge: in ADJACENT cabinet, never behind the fridge; sink: high on sidewall away from trap drips; kamado: near cradle. Anything near the propane bay mounts HIGH — gas pools low.

items.spec: PVC WP j-box, 4" square class, ¾" hubs
items.note: Accessible splice hub in the fridge cavity — a removable panel counts as accessible; buried behind tile doesn't.

items.spec: 12 AWG stranded THWN-2 — black + red (hots), white ×2 or shared per fill (neutrals), green (ground)
items.note: Measure each conduit path end-to-end, add 10% + box tails. Two 20A circuits: (1) fridge dedicated, (2) grill + kamado + sink convenience. Pull, coil tails, land NOTHING until Phase 8. Remember D20: a future point-of-use water heater (~12A) weighs on circuit loading.

items.spec: Rubber desk-type grommet, ~1" hole
items.note: Fridge cord pass-through from cavity to adjacent-bay outlet; steel wool + sealant close it at Phase 8 — pest highway otherwise.

items.spec: ½" × 3'×5' sheets (Durock/Hardie)
items.note: Guide's own caveat: the open back removed ~100 sq ft from earlier estimates — RE-COUNT against the final plan before buying, then buy 2–3 extra. Grill-bay back sheet gets pre-attached to its panel at Phase 1 tilt-up. Respirator for every cut — non-negotiable.

items.spec: Power Pro #9 × 1¼" ceramic-coated wafer-head backer screws, 925-count box
items.note: Covers the whole project. 1¼" is right: ½" board + ¾" past it to thread the steel. Screws every 8" into studs.

items.spec: 2" × 150' alkali-resistant fiberglass mesh
items.note: All seams + corners, plus the curved wall's facet joints (Step 3.5.2).

items.spec: Polymer-modified thinset, 50 lb bag
items.note: Seam taping, facet packing, and laminating alternate on the curve. Cladding thinset is a separate Phase 4 buy.

items.spec: RedGard or AquaDefense, 1-gal pails
items.note: Counter deck (top + back edge) + clad faces ONLY — two coats, opposing directions. Boarded back gets NO membrane (drying side by design, D24). Clad within 2–4 months — UV degrades it, rain doesn't.

items.spec: Galvanized or stainless Z-profile sized to the backsplash top plate width
items.note: Across the backsplash top plate, sloped toward the fence, front edge caulked. Measure top-plate length + lap allowance.

items.spec: Stucco weep screed for ~1" finish stack, 10' sticks
items.note: Base perimeter of all clad faces, ~½" above pavers (D17 — no raw wood at grade). Measure clad perimeter LF.

items.spec: ¼" mesh galvanized hardware cloth, 36" × 25' roll
items.note: Entire back EXCEPT the grill bay; kamado corner stays cloth (D14). Cavity height ~33" fits 36" width; measure back LF minus grill bay. Curved corner gets stapled progressively during Phase 1 framing — buy before framing if you want it on hand then.

items.spec: #8 stainless pan-heads + stainless fender washers, small box each
items.note: Cloth 'staples/screws on from the front through the bays'.

items.spec: R-15 mineral wool batt pack
items.note: Guide's Phase 3 list: '1 pack does grill bay + fridge bay + sink back.' Note Step 3.4 separately demotes the grill-bay-adjacent cavity batt to a buy-it-later retrofit — flagged for review; reconcile before checkout.

items.spec: ¼" × 3'×5' sheets
items.note: Cut into vertical strips ~6" wide, one per arc stud bay — flat facets, zero bending stress. Two backer screws per strip per stud.

items.spec: Galvanized expanded-metal lath, 2.5 lb, 27"×96" sheets
items.note: Wrap the arc continuously, laps 2"+, fasten into every stud — this is the true curve former.

items.spec: Polymer-modified stucco base coat (or sanded thinset), 50 lb bag
items.note: Screed to the true radius with a plywood template band-sawed from the SketchUp arc; two thin passes beat one thick.

items.spec: Smooth stucco finish coat, 1 bag/pail
items.note: If the curve finishes as the smooth accent band (the guide's lean); narrow mosaic/picket tile is the named alternative — skip this if tile wins.

items.spec: Stainless louvered island vents — SIZE TO THE COYOTE MANUAL'S SQUARE-INCH NUMBERS (LP compartments have explicit low-vent requirements)
items.note: Toe-kick under the grill (LOW — propane sinks) + island ends. The manual is law — buy after the cutout/vent sheet is in hand.

items.spec: Plug-in or battery propane (LP) gas detector
items.note: Mounts LOW in the propane bay. If the grill ever converts to NG, add detection HIGH — detection flips at conversion.

items.spec: 100% silicone, exterior-rated, 10 oz tubes
items.note: Seal every penetration cut BEFORE hanging: conduit, PEX, the drain-riser hole in the cabinet floor board, vent sleeves.

items.spec: ½" cement board offcut + non-combustible standoffs
items.note: Only if the Coyote manual's rear clearance number is tight against the boarded back + 2" fence gap — the guide's named cheap fix. Check the manual first; likely free from sheet offcuts.

items.spec: RECTIFIED porcelain plank, light driftwood tone, ~48" length class
items.note: ~135 sq ft faces is the guide figure — re-measure faces before ordering. Extra box = attic stock; dye lots disappear. Dry-lay the entire face first; door edges land on grout joints. ⚠ If you want one combined tile order, the backsplash pick (Phase 7) practically decides now.

items.spec: Tuscan/Raimondi/MLT clips (1/16" or match plank joint) + wedges
items.note: Non-negotiable on planks. Wedges reuse; clips are consumable — count from the dry-lay if you want it tighter.

items.spec: Polymer-modified exterior-rated thinset, 50 lb bags, white
items.note: Back-butter EVERY plank + combed wall = double coverage, ~30 sq ft/bag effective over ~135 sq ft. Same pass beds the Schluter trim legs.

items.spec: 100% epoxy grout (SpectraLOCK Pro class), color dead-matched to plank
items.note: Joints read as reveals. Clean haze aggressively as you go — epoxy haze is forever. Rectified tight joints keep consumption low; confirm coverage chart against joint width.

items.spec: Schluter Quadec or Rondec, height matched to plank thickness (~3/8"), 8' 2½" lengths, finish of your pick
items.note: EVERY outside corner — never miter porcelain. Also the plank-stop trim line where the field meets the curved wall (D22). Measure vertical corner LF + curve termination.

items.spec: 100% silicone matched to grout color (Latasil class), 10 oz
items.note: Movement joints: every change of plane, cladding-to-counter/appliance/trim. Silicone, not grout — law.

items.spec: 1"×1" aluminum angle or 1" thin-wall square tube, 8' lengths
items.note: Frame = opening minus an even 1/8–3/16" reveal; center rail on anything over ~24". Take perimeter LF from actual door openings. Cut with the non-ferrous blade.

items.spec: Flat aluminum gussets or 90° corner brackets to suit 1" stock
items.note: Corners joined with gussets + stainless machine screws + nylock nuts PLUS structural adhesive — bolt-and-bond, no welding.

items.spec: #10-24 stainless machine screws assorted lengths + nylock nuts, small boxes
items.note: Frame corners AND hinge mounting — machine screws + lock nuts into the frame, NEVER self-tappers, for connections carrying the door's whole life.

items.spec: 3M 5200 (cartridge) or 3M DP420 (dual cartridge + mixing nozzles)
items.note: Guide names both products. The bond + bolts make the torsion box.

items.spec: GoBoard/Wedi foam backer, ½" × 3'×5' sheets
items.note: NOT cement board — door weight kills hinges; foam bonds porcelain equally well. Count from door areas. Bond with thinset/adhesive AND screw with stainless self-tappers.

items.spec: Waterproof board sealant (GoBoard Pro Sealant class), 1 tube
items.note: Waterproof the backer edges/seams per the JM install guide in the reference library.

items.spec: #8 stainless self-tappers, ~¾", small box
items.note: Backer to frame, alongside the adhesive bond.

items.spec: Concealed hinges made for aluminum-frame cabinet doors — clamp-on type, NO 35mm cup bore, 3-way adjustable (search 'aluminum frame door concealed hinge' / glass-cabinet hardware)
items.note: ⚠ Decide BEFORE tiling door faces. Stainless piano hinge (cut to door height) is the sag-proof fallback if clamp-ons don't suit the frame profile. Phase 1 backing supports either.

items.spec: Stainless bar/pull handles, through-bolted
items.note: Diamond-drill through tile into the frame, sleeve holes with silicone, through-bolt.

items.spec: Outdoor-rated magnetic or roller catch
items.note: Hang after cladding; dial reveals even.

items.spec: .050–.063" aluminum sheet OR 3–4mm ACP/Dibond panels
items.note: From the drawer cut list: each box = opening − slide clearance (usually ½" per side). Build the tallest drawer first as the pattern.

items.spec: 1"×1" aluminum angle (corners) + aluminum flat bar (slide-height rails), 8' lengths
items.note: Angle is the frame, panel is the skin; flat-bar rails give solid bite for slide screws and stiffen the wall in one move.

items.spec: 1/8" stainless pop rivets, 250-count
items.note: Every corner joint. $25 rivet gun is on the tool list. Stainless (not aluminum) stays the rule near the firebox surround.

items.spec: Perforated aluminum, ~.050", cut to drawer bottoms
items.note: Bottoms drain instead of pooling.

items.spec: U-channel sized to panel thickness (.050–4mm)
items.note: Top rim of each box — no razor edges, looks finished.

items.spec: Stainless full-extension ball-bearing slides, length to bay depth (typ. 20–22")
items.note: Boxes land under 10 lb empty — standard slides carry them. Mount to flat-bar rails and to Phase 1 blocking/angle on the cabinet side; size box to the slide spec's clearance.

items.spec: ¼" stainless bolts + fender washers + nylocks, assorted
items.note: Faces are tiled ½" cement-board panels (Phase 3 offcuts) through-bolted from inside with SLOTTED holes for alignment.

items.spec: Light-colored elastomeric/masonry paint (guide-specified type), 1 gal
items.note: Over interior cement board so you can see into the bays. Quantity is my estimate.

items.spec: Stainless wire shelving or marine HDPE sheet, cut to bays
items.note: Sits on the Phase 1 blocking/ledgers. Both picks drain — that's why they won.

items.spec: LIGHT granite (Alaska White / Kashmir White / Colonial White / Bianco Antico class), 3cm (1¼" nominal / 1-3/16" actual), LEATHERED — two pieces, seam at the corner
items.note: Remnants $10–18/sq ft + $600–1,000 fabrication → $1,000–1,800 all-in. Dark stone hits 150–170°F — off the table. Seam away from the sink; match 3cm across both pieces; cutouts need meat — constrains which remnants work. Give fabricator: sink template, grill cutout (SL30 25½×20½ / SL36 32½×20½ — decided by now), Big Joe A–R chart opening, seam location. ⚠ Backsplash tile must be chosen before templating (rear-overhang detail).

items.spec: Penetrating stone sealer, ~$25 bottle
items.note: Seal at install, then twice a year, 20 minutes — calendar it.

items.spec: 100% silicone, clear or color-matched, 10 oz
items.note: Counters set in silicone beds; fabricator epoxies + color-matches the seam and often supplies setting silicone — confirm before buying.

items.spec: Polymer-modified thinset (Phase 4 leftover works)
items.note: Mortar-bed the kamado feet zone and the throat-band 'little shelf' so the stone is fully bedded and never spans air (Step 6.3).

items.spec: Smooth light porcelain, 24"+ faces, minimal grout lines
items.note: Grill zone ~4' + kamado corner ~3' of the 18" band. Grout behind a grill is a grease trap — big smooth tile is the wipeable substitute for the stainless panel that was ruled out. Kamado corner may alternatively take the feature tile (soot-on-grout tradeoff, guide's words).

items.spec: Your pick of the four comps — Sea Glass Sage is the standing recommendation; porcelain or glass ONLY behind cook surfaces
items.qty: ~20 sq ft (incl. the guide's +20% picket waste) + 1 extra box
items.note: Sink wall + runs, ~11' of the 18" band. Picket shapes waste more. Extra box = attic stock. ⚠ Deadline was Phase 6 templating — must be locked by now.

items.spec: 100% epoxy grout, ONE SHADE DARKER than instinct
items.note: Pale grout behind a grill grays in a season anyway — guide's rule.

items.spec: Schluter-type profile matched to tile thickness, 8' 2½" lengths
items.note: Smooth-to-feature transitions and any change of plane/material — metal trim + silicone, never a grouted butt joint. Measure transition LF.

items.spec: Polymer-modified exterior thinset (Phase 4 leftover works)
items.note: Same one-material-discipline system as the cladding.

items.spec: 100% silicone matched to grout, 10 oz
items.note: Counter-to-backsplash joint gets silicone, NEVER grout — it's a movement joint.

items.spec: C3SL30 (cutout 25½"W × 20½"D × 10¼"H, ~$3,100 LP) or C3SL36 (32½"W × 20½"D × 10¼"H, ~$3,500–3,700) — LP or NG, ships fuel-specific
items.note: Size was decided back at Phase 1 framing; fuel: tie-in a year+ out → order LP, cook on the tank, budget the Coyote LP→NG conversion kit + orifice/regulator swap (~$60–100) for tie-in day. Order early enough that the manual is in hand for Phase 3 vent sizing — the manual is law. Flange bears on counter, sealed per instructions.

items.spec: Big Joe Series III grill head, no cart
items.note: ~$2,200–2,700. Assemble IN PLACE — it does not fit through openings after the fact; 400+ lb ceramic, two-strong-people minimum, rent/borrow a shoulder or appliance dolly.

items.spec: KJ ceramic feet for built-in installs
items.note: 2 front, 2 back, spaced so it can't rock when the dome swings. Required in built-ins — they create the under-grill airflow; never set the ceramic base flat on the deck. kamadojoe.com parts.

items.spec: SMOBOT KJ version, $349, micro-USB powered
items.note: KJ version ONLY — the standard BGE daisy-wheel kit does NOT fit the cast-aluminum Kontrol Tower. Routes to the cradle outlet.

items.spec: Looftlighter electric fire starter, ~$80
items.note: Covers the manual-ignition gap; stages at the cradle outlet with the SMOBOT.

items.spec: Velieta 24" front-venting built-in/freestanding, compressor, adjustable feet, ~$300–450
items.note: ORDER EARLY — the Phase 1 bay gets framed to its actual cut sheet on arrival. Front grille completely unobstructed at install; cord through the grommeted pass-through to the adjacent-bay outlet.

items.spec: Stainless bar sink + cold-only faucet + permanently attachable sink cover
items.note: $120–250 class. Buy early enough to hand the fabricator the sink template at Phase 6. The cover satisfies the sewered-outdoor-sink rain rule for tie-in day. Set in silicone per template.

items.spec: 20A, weather-resistant (WR) + tamper-resistant, self-testing GFCI
items.note: Head of each circuit per the guide's 'GFCI everything — at the head of each circuit or breaker-level.' If you go breaker-level GFCI instead, swap these for 2 WR duplexes and 2 GFCI breakers. Test every GFCI.

items.spec: 20A WR/TR duplex receptacles
items.note: Downstream outlets on circuit 2 (kamado + sink convenience), protected by the head GFCI.

items.spec: Weatherproof in-use ('bubble') covers, single-gang, sized to box + plug
items.note: Guide requires in-use covers on everything; count matches the four outlet locations. Covers stay closed over cords at the grill outlet.

items.spec: Preprinted circuit label kit or label-maker tape
items.note: Label the circuits at trim-out — guide's electrical-trim step.

items.spec: 20 lb LP cylinder, filled
items.note: Sits on the pavers in the open, unfinished under-grill bay. Skip if you already own one. (Only if the grill ordered LP.)

items.spec: Spray bottle + dish soap, or commercial gas leak detector solution
items.note: Leak-test EVERY propane fitting with soapy water — guide's safety language, verbatim requirement.

items.spec: 1½" AAV + adapter to the trap arm
items.note: Guide: 'AAV under the counter if that's your vent plan' — conditional on the venting approach at drain trim/tie-in.

items.spec: ½" PEX × faucet thread adapter or ½" quarter-turn stop + braided connector
items.note: Hooks the cold faucet to the Phase −1 PEX run; match your PEX connection system.

items.spec: Coarse steel wool pad + exterior sealant tube
items.note: Seal the fridge cord pass-through — 'pest highway otherwise.' Silicone leftovers cover the rest of the punch-list change-of-plane joints.

assumptions: Pipe is sold in 10' sticks: the guide's 2" ABS drain run and riser fit one stick; the 18–24" gas sleeve and spare chase both come from one 2" Sch 40 PVC stick. Guide allows 3" PVC for the gas sleeve ('luxurious clearance') — I specced 2" since the guide confirms 2" ID passes ¾–1" CSST with fittings.
Drain elbow: one long-sweep 90 is the primary buy; the two-45s alternative is noted on the same line rather than double-bought. Never a tight vent elbow, per the guide.
ABS over PVC for the drain per the guide's 'San Diego is traditionally ABS territory' — the owner must still match (or transition-cement) at tie-in day.
Sleeve/chase caps specced as dry-fit/taped so they come off for the future pull; the guide says only 'cap both ends.' Drain caps glue on (riser cap gets cut to height at trim-out).
P-trap, tailpiece, trap adapter, jug and bulkhead are bought in Phase −1 because Step 6 says to hard-pipe the trap now; bulkhead size is my call — the guide gives no dimension.
PT 2×4 length assumed 8' (guide only gives the 8–10 board count); take a cut list from the actual footprint before buying.
Tapcon spec (¼" × 3¼", ~25 count) derived from 1½" PT + ≥1" paver embedment and 'every few feet' over ~60 LF of sill; the guide names no size or count.
Sill-seal roll count (2 × 50') assumes ~60–100 LF of sill runs; return the surplus.

Electrical conduit assumed ¾" Sch 40 with 12 AWG THWN-2 for the two 20A circuits; the guide states neither trade size nor wire gauge. Offset/coupling size must match the existing stubs — measure before buying.
Box count (4 outlets + 1 j-box) and in-use cover count (4) are derived from the guide's four named outlet locations plus the fridge-cavity splice hub.

GFCI plan assumed device-level: one GFCI at the head of each circuit + 2 protected WR duplexes downstream. The guide's alternative (breaker-level GFCI) changes this to 2 GFCI breakers + 4 WR duplexes.
Flagged, not fixed: #8 × ½" TEK math — the guide's single 500-count box vs ~4 screws × 150 cut studs = ~600. Kept the guide's 1 box verbatim with a buy-a-second-if-cutting-all-fifty note.

Flagged, not fixed: Rockwool R-15 — the Phase 3 materials list says buy 1 pack now ('grill bay + fridge bay + sink back') while Step 3.4 defers the grill-bay-adjacent batt to an if-needed retrofit. Listed 1 pack per the materials list with the conflict noted for owner review.
Blocking, ledgers, boxed members, and the doubled-rail kamado grid are cut from the ~50 stud / ~14 track sticks — no separate steel purchase line.

Hardware-cloth roll (36" × 25') sized from ~33" cavity height and ~18 LF back minus the ~4' grill bay; verify against the real back before buying. Its stainless screw/washer attachment hardware is my addition — the guide says only 'staples/screws on from the front.'
Vent quantity (~4) is a placeholder — the guide's rule is to size vent area to the Coyote manual's square-inch numbers, so the real buy waits for the manual.

Cladding consumables derived from the guide's ~135 sq ft: 5 bags thinset (back-butter doubles coverage), ~400 leveling clips, 2 epoxy grout units, 3 silicone tubes. All are stocking estimates, not guide figures.

Door/drawer quantities (angle stock, GoBoard sheets, hinges at 2/door, handles, catches, slides, panel stock) depend on the final door/drawer count and opening sizes, which the guide never enumerates — all marked measure.
Structural adhesive marked source:guide because the guide names the exact products (3M 5200 / DP420); cartridge count is mine.
Cabinet paint type (light elastomeric/masonry) is the guide's; the 1-gal quantity is mine, so it is marked estimate.

Phase 8 big-ticket items carry order-early notes that are mine: the grill manual must exist before Phase 3 vent sizing and Phase 1 bay framing; the fridge cut sheet before its Phase 1 bay; the sink template before Phase 6 templating. The guide implies each but files them under Phase 8 purchasing.
Propane tank (20 lb) included on the assumption the owner doesn't already own one, and only applies if the open LP-vs-NG decision lands LP.
Fence standoff heat shield and stucco finish coat are conditional lines, kept because the guide names them as contingent buys (tight rear clearance; smooth-stucco curve finish).
Consumable counts throughout (mesh tape rolls, silicone tubes, cement cans, sand bags, rivet boxes) are shopping-cart stocking estimates sized to the described work, not guide numbers.

Tools are excluded — the guide keeps its own tool list (own/buy/rent) and this list is materials only. The pull-string spool, Sharpie labeling, and plywood screed template ride on existing tool-list items or scrap.
